skill.name: azure-microsoft-playwright-testing-ts
skill.description: Run Playwright tests at scale using Azure Playwright Workspaces with cloud-hosted browsers and Azure portal reporting for TypeScript projects
skill.summary: Scale Playwright browser tests across Azure cloud infrastructure with Entra ID authentication and integrated test reporting
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 60 patterns but all are false positives from Markdown documentation. The skill contains TypeScript code examples for Azure Playwright Testing SDK. External command patterns are npm/bash examples in code blocks, not executable code. Network and environment variable access are legitimate Azure authentication patterns required for the service.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains External Service URLs
low_findings.description: The skill documentation includes Azure service endpoint URLs and references to external Microsoft APIs. These are legitimate service endpoints for Azure Playwright Workspaces but users should verify URLs match their workspace configuration.

content.user_title: Run Playwright Tests on Azure Cloud Browsers
content.value_statement: Scale your end-to-end browser tests across Azure's cloud-hosted browsers without managing infrastructure. Integrate with CI/CD pipelines and view results in Azure portal with built-in reporting.
actual_capabilities: Configure Azure Playwright Workspaces with Entra ID or managed identity authentication
Run Playwright tests in parallel across 20+ cloud-hosted browser instances
Integrate with GitHub Actions and Azure Pipelines for CI/CD test execution
Enable test artifacts including traces and videos for failure analysis
View test results and reports in Azure portal dashboard
limitations: Requires Azure Playwright Workspace subscription and configuration
Needs Azure AD credentials or service principal for authentication
Cloud browser tests require network access to Azure endpoints
Package migration required for users of retired @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing

use_cases.title: Enterprise QA Team Scaling Tests
use_cases.description: Run large test suites across multiple browser configurations in parallel without maintaining local browser infrastructure. Reduce test execution time from hours to minutes.

use_cases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Integration
use_cases.description: Add cloud-hosted browser testing to GitHub Actions or Azure Pipelines with automatic authentication and result reporting in Azure portal.

use_cases.title: Cross-Browser Compatibility Testing
use_cases.description: Execute tests across Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit browsers hosted on Azure infrastructure with consistent environments.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Azure Playwright Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up Azure Playwright Testing for my TypeScript project. I need to configure the package with Entra ID authentication and run my existing Playwright tests on cloud browsers.

prompt_templates.title: Configure CI/CD Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs Playwright tests on Azure Playwright Workspaces. Include Azure login step, environment variables for the service URL, and parallel test execution with 20 workers.

prompt_templates.title: Migration from Old Package
prompt_templates.prompt: My project uses the deprecated @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing package. Help me migrate to @azure/playwright by updating the configuration, authentication, and reporter imports.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Configuration with Artifacts
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure Azure Playwright Testing with trace collection on retries, video recording on failures, and custom run names for the Azure portal. Use ServiceOS.LINUX and set connection timeout to 30 seconds.

output_examples.input: Set up Azure Playwright Testing with default configuration
output_examples.output: Creates playwright.service.config.ts with createAzurePlaywrightConfig, DefaultAzureCredential for authentication, and HTML reporter enabled before Azure reporter for proper output rendering.

output_examples.input: Run tests with 20 parallel workers on Azure
output_examples.output: Executes npx playwright test --config=playwright.service.config.ts --workers=20, distributing tests across cloud-hosted browsers and displaying results in Azure portal.

best_practices: Use Entra ID authentication with explicit credential parameter instead of access tokens for better security
List HTML reporter before Azure reporter in configuration to ensure proper report generation
Enable trace and video artifacts for debugging failures with trace set to on-first-retry
anti_patterns: Do not hardcode access tokens in configuration files - use environment variables or Azure Key Vault
Avoid running tests without sufficient workers - underutilizes Azure's parallel execution capability
Do not skip HTML reporter when using Azure reporter - Azure reporter depends on HTML reporter output

faq.question: What is the difference between @azure/playwright and @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing?
faq.answer: The @azure/microsoft-playwright-testing package is retired as of March 8, 2026. The new @azure/playwright package requires explicit credential parameters and uses createAzurePlaywrightConfig instead of getServiceConfig.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Playwright Workspaces?
faq.answer: Use Entra ID authentication with DefaultAzureCredential for local development or ManagedIdentityCredential for CI/CD. Authenticate with az login before running tests locally.

faq.question: What Playwright version do I need?
faq.answer: Playwright 1.47 or higher is required for basic usage. Azure reporter features require Playwright 1.57 or higher.

faq.question: How many parallel workers can I use?
faq.answer: Azure Playwright Workspaces supports 20 or more parallel workers depending on your workspace configuration. Use --workers=20 or higher for optimal parallel execution.

faq.question: Can I use this with existing Playwright tests?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill wraps your existing Playwright configuration. Import your base config and extend it with createAzurePlaywrightConfig without modifying individual test files.

faq.question: How do I view test results after execution?
faq.answer: Test results appear in Azure portal under your Playwright Workspace. Configure the Azure reporter and list HTML reporter first for local HTML reports.
